MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOSEPH LUCIA, ESQ., RAINS LUCIA STERN, PC, 925.609.1699, FOR PLAINITFF DARYL BERKSON BRAND INDIVIDUALLY AND AS QUALIFIED SURVIVOR OF WILLIAM BRAND, DECEASED; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and ANTHONY HARGE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 and denied as to issues 5 and 7. Plaintiff has not opposed or, at least not meaningfully opposed, issues 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 and defendants have satisfied their burden of production as to those issues. Plaintiff produced evidence showing that there is a triable issue whether Defendant Harge acted negligently, thereby requiring the denial of the motions as to issues 5 and 7. Specifically, there are triable issues whether Harge was travelling at an excessive rate of speed, he failed to stop, he failed to ring the gong, and he was texting at the time of the accident. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
